examples.title: Check if Function is Injective
examples.description: Algorithm to check injectivity for finite sets using brute force comparison
examples.explanation: Compare every pair of outputs. If any two different inputs produce the same output, function is not injective.

examples.title: Check if Function is Surjective
examples.description: Algorithm to verify surjectivity by checking all codomain elements have pre-images
examples.explanation: Iterate through domain, mark each output in visited set. After processing all inputs, if visited equals B, function is surjective.

examples.title: Compute Function Composition
examples.description: Algorithm to compute composition of two functions f and g
examples.code:
FUNCTION compose(f, g, A, C):
 // f: B→C, g: A→B
 // Result: h: A→C where h(x) = f(g(x))
 h ← empty function
 FOR each x IN A:
 y ← g(x) // Get intermediate value
 z ← f(y) // Apply f to get final value
 h(x) ← z
 RETURN h
examples.explanation: For each input x in domain A, first apply g to get intermediate value y, then apply f to y to get final composition result.

examples.title: Find Inverse of Bijective Function
examples.description: Algorithm to compute inverse when function is known to be bijective
examples.code:
FUNCTION findInverse(f, A, B):
 // f: A→B is bijective
 // Returns f⁻¹: B→A
 inv ← empty function
 FOR each x IN A:
 y ← f(x)
 inv(y) ← x // Store pre-image
 RETURN inv
examples.explanation: Since f is bijective, each y in B has exactly one pre-image x in A. Build inverse by mapping each output back to its unique input.
